description_long: This sculpture depicts a partial human male torso, rendered in a dark, metallic material with a smooth yet subtly textured surface. The figure is missing its head, arms, and lower legs, focusing attention on the muscular anatomy of the chest, abdomen, and upper thighs. The stance is upright, with one leg slightly forward and the other merging into a rough, abstract base that anchors the figure to a square plinth. The back reveals detailed musculature and a naturalistic curvature of the spine, while the sides show the transition from the realistic form to the more abstract, unfinished base. The entire piece has a reflective, bronze-like finish, giving it a sense of weight and permanence. The sculpture stands on a simple, flat pedestal that contrasts with the organic form above.
description: A dark metallic sculpture of a partial male torso, missing head and arms, with detailed musculature and a reflective finish, mounted on a square plinth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muscular chest, abdomen, and the forward stance of the left leg.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the torso and the abstract, rough base merging with the leg.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back muscles, spine, and the transition from realistic form to the abstract base.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the curvature of the torso and the integration of the figure into the base.
attribute_location_context: No visible environmental context; the sculpture is shown isolated on a neutral background.
